name: Stir-Fried Rice-Stick Noodles
recipeIngredient: 1 small Onion, cut into thin slices
1 Stalk Celery, cut into diagonal thin slices
1/2 Green bell pepper, seeded and sliced thin
2 tbsp Indian Madras curry powder (or to taste)
1 tbsp Light soy sauce (or more if needed)
recipeInstructions:
1. In two separate medium bowls, cover rice-stick noodles and mushrooms with warm water for 20 minutes or until soft and pliable. Drain the noodles and squeeze out excess water; set aside. Drain mushrooms and remove stems at the base. Cut caps into thin slices; set aside.
2. Cut chicken breast into 1/3-inch-thick strips; pat dry. Slice barbecued pork into 1/4-inch-thick julienne strips; pat dry. Shell and devein shrimp; pat dry.
3. Heat a wok over medium heat until hot. Add 1 1/2 tablespoons of oil and half the ginger and salt; gently sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
4. Increase heat to high and add chicken, stirring-frying for a few seconds. Toss in shrimp and stir-fry together until firm to the touch. Mix in barbecued pork; remove and set aside.
5. Add remaining oil, ginger, and salt to the hot wok. When oil is hot, add mushrooms, onion, celery, snow peas, and bell pepper; stir-fry until tender and crisp for 1-2 minutes.
6. Mix in sugar, curry powder, soy sauces, and half the chicken stock. Add rice-stick noodles; use chopsticks or tongs to lift, shake, and separate strands of noodles until evenly coated with seasonings.
7. Continue stir-frying another minute until noodles are moist and begin to cling together. If needed, add more chicken stock to prevent dryness.
8. Add reserved meat mixture and green onions; toss together. Taste and adjust seasoning as desired. Transfer to a serving platter and serve hot. Serves 4 as a light lunch.
